title: How the Fight Over Israel Is Playing Out Inside MAGA
summary: The war in Iran has added to a tectonic shift in public opinion — a bipartisan swing away from Israel. Some on the far-right are fighting to keep President Trump’s movement aligned with the Jewish state.

title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
summary: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.

title: Iran’s Oil Sector and Economy Are Under Pressure as U.S. Blockade Bites
summary: Tehran says it can endure the pain, but its oil may soon have nowhere to go.

title: Macron Calls for U.S. and Iran to Open Strait of Hormuz While They Negotiate
summary: Emmanuel Macron, the French president, hopes the Strait of Hormuz can reopen even before the U.S.-Iran war formally ends. His plan has many pitfalls.

title: Higher Gas Prices Are Hitting Lower-Income Americans the Hardest
summary: People with lower incomes are driving less and devoting more of their budgets to transport as gas prices soar.

title: Why Is Gas So Much More Expensive in Some States Than Others?
summary: Shipping, taxes, competition and environmental regulations all play a part in the cost of gasoline. That can lead to stark differences in pump prices.

title: Oil Prices Fall on Hope U.S.-Iran War Turmoil in the Gulf May Be Easing
summary: Oil prices fell and stocks rose after President Trump’s about-face to pause the U.S. operation to escort commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz

title: Ted Turner, Creator of CNN and the 24-Hour News Cycle, Dies at 87
summary: As one of the most important figures in media history, he oversaw a vast cable empire of news, sports and entertainment channels.

title: Rupert Murdoch, David Zaslav and More Media Moguls Remember Ted Turner, CNN’s Founder
summary: The CNN founder’s death was met with encomiums from leaders in the media industry, which he helped reinvent. “He will be remembered for generations,” said one.

title: Tennessee Republicans Unveil New Congressional Map Carving Up Majority-Black House District
summary: The General Assembly is expected to quickly approve the map, which slices up Memphis, a majority-Black city that makes up most of the state’s lone Democratic district.

title: Obama Talks Trump, Mamdani, Aliens and More in Colbert Interview
summary: The former president was critical of President Trump, without ever naming him. Mr. Obama also urged Democrats to be plain-spoken, and talked about aliens.

title: Candidates for California Governor Debate Tonight: What to Know
summary: Seven candidates in the California governor’s race will face off at a debate hosted by NBC Los Angeles. The New York Times will livestream the event.

title: Tofersen, a New Treatment for A.L.S., Reverses Symptoms for Some
summary: The drug is for a small subset of patients. But evidence that breathing and strength can get better for some of them is remarkable for a paralyzing, usually fatal disease.

title: Weather Service Races to Rehire as Storm Season Arrives
summary: After deep cuts last year, the agency is hiring hundreds. But fears linger that it isn’t equipped for imminent tornado and hurricane threats.

title: Howard Lutnick Faces Questions From Congress About Epstein Ties
summary: The commerce secretary appeared in a closed-door session on Wednesday with the House Oversight Committee

title: Conservative Group’s Connections at the State Department Raise Some Alarms
summary: Founders of the Ben Franklin Fellowship are trying to dismantle pro-diversity practices in the agency and to boost career diplomats who promote President Trump’s ideas.

title: Her Self-Experiment with Drug Detox Almost Broke Her
summary: Against expert advice, people are using new and unpredictable synthetic drugs to experiment on themselves in hopes of becoming free of addiction.

title: A Look Inside the Case That Enshrined Political Power for Billionaires
summary: After Watergate, Congress tried to curtail the role of money in politics. But a pivotal Supreme Court case nipped it in the bud. Years later, new details are emerging on how wealthy Americans were conferred with a “right to spend” on elections.

title: Leo XIV Is the First Pope From the U.S. He’s Making That an Asset.
summary: A year into his papacy, Leo has shown a readiness to challenge Washington, defying any concerns about the election of an American pontiff.

title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
summary: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.

title: The Longevity Secrets Helping Athletes Blow Past the Limits of Age
summary: With cutting-edge sports medicine and sci-fi gadgetry, more and more athletes are figuring out how to extend their careers.

title: 3 People Are Evacuated From Cruise Ship With Hantavirus Outbreak
summary: Three people with possible symptoms of the disease were evacuated on Wednesday morning from the Dutch-flagged MV Hondius.

title: Guy Goma’s Accidental BBC Interview Lives On After 20 Years
summary: Guy Goma thought he was interviewing for an I.T. job when he found himself on air on the BBC. What came next was familiar to anyone who has been unprepared at work.

title: Artifacts from the Titanic May Soon Be Auctioned
summary: The last time a sale was proposed, the idea drew objections from the U.S. and French governments, and from UNESCO and other cultural institutions.

title: In ‘Rocky Horror,’ Luke Evans Finds His Ballad of Sexual Liberation
summary: What does it take to play Frank-N-Furter in “The Rocky Horror Show” on Broadway? Fishnets, five-inch heels, and an endless supply of glitter.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson: Give Us the Aliens
summary: Is that too much to ask for?

title: John Roberts Believes in an America That Doesn’t Exist
summary: Guess who benefits from a “colorblind Constitution.”

title: Tell Us How High Gas Prices Have Affected Your Finances
summary: With fuel costs soaring from the war with Iran, we want to hear how you’re feeling about the U.S. economy.

title: His DNA Was Taken After His Arrest at an ICE Protest. Now, He’s Suing.
summary: In a lawsuit, an Air Force veteran says that the federal government is sending a “chilling message” to people demonstrating against the immigration crackdown.

title: Office of Virginia State Senator Louise Lucas Raided by F.B.I. Agents
summary: Two people familiar with the case said the search of a Democratic lawmaker’s office was related to a Biden-era investigation of possible corruption and bribery related to marijuana businesses.

title: Reclaiming the Name of the Black Hero Who Inspired ‘Uncle Tom’s Cabin’
summary: After 42 years of slavery, Josiah Henson escaped to Canada, where he wrote a memoir, founded a school and led others to freedom. But his home long bore the name “Uncle Tom,” to the offense of many.

title: A Democratic State Auditor Is Taking on Her Party, and Firing Up Voters
summary: Massachusetts voters approved a ballot measure in 2024 authorizing Diana DiZoglio, the state auditor, to closely scrutinize the State Legislature. A battle ensued.
